questions: The `buildBasicModePhaseConfig` wrapper in `modelPhase.ts` divides motion by 100 before passing to the core builder. When inlining into the handler, should we call the wrapper (which returns `{ model, phaseConfig }`) or the core builder from `phaseConfig.ts` directly? The handler currently uses the wrapper via `buildBasicModeGenerationRequest` — plan assumes we keep using it for consistency.
Are there any other callers of `usePresetAutoSelect` besides `useMotionControlPresetState`? The grep found only one call site, but confirming there are no dynamic/conditional usages elsewhere.

success_criteria.criterion: handleGenerationTypeModeChange writes generationTypeMode, selectedPhasePresetId, and phaseConfig in a single updateFields call (no separate rebuildPhaseConfig call)
usePresetAutoSelect no longer fires on generationTypeMode changes — only on initial mount and shot navigation
User LoRAs are correctly merged into phaseConfig when switching I2V ↔ VACE (verified by reading the built config)
All existing tests pass (npx vitest run)
handleRestoreDefaults also uses atomic updateFields instead of separate rebuildPhaseConfig call
rebuildPhaseConfig remains available for handleAmountOfMotionChange and handleSelectedLorasChange (incremental updates)
Manual smoke test: toggle I2V/VACE with LoRAs selected, verify phaseConfig includes user LoRAs and preset ID matches mode

assumptions: There is only one call site for usePresetAutoSelect (in useMotionControlPresetState). No other component directly invokes it.
The updateFields method on shotSettingsRef batches all field updates into a single state write (no intermediate renders between fields).

The buildBasicModeGenerationRequest wrapper in modelPhase.ts (which divides motion by 100) is the correct function to use when building phaseConfig in the handler, matching current rebuildPhaseConfig behavior.
Advanced mode users who toggle I2V/VACE should still get a force-rebuilt phaseConfig (current behavior), since the phase structure fundamentally changes (2 vs 3 phases).
